data.title: UiPath CEO Dines Daniel Sells 90,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameDines Daniel
RelationshipCEO and Chairman, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$1,484,113
SEC FilingForm 4

UiPath's CEO and Chairman, Dines Daniel, a 10% owner and director of the company, sold a total of 90,000 shares of Class A Common Stock over two transactions on December 22 and 23, 2025, for a total sale amount of $1,484,113. The sales were executed at weighted average prices of $16.4911 and $16.4892 respectively, under a qualified selling plan in compliance with Rule 10b5-1. Following these transactions, Daniel directly owns 28,703,585 shares and indirectly owns 9,615,297 shares through Ice Vulcan Holding Limited, maintaining sole voting and investment power.

SEC Filing: UiPath, Inc. [ PATH ] - Form 4 - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 2130 GMT on Tuesday:

** Nestle said it has completed the sale of its remaining 40% stake in packaged meat business Herta Foods to Spanish company Casa Tarradellas.

** ServiceNow <NOW.N> has agreed to buy cybersecurity startup Armis for $7.75 billion in cash, the companies said, as the enterprise software maker looks to attract new customers amid growing risks of cyberattacks.

** SiriusPoint subsidiary International Medical Group (IMG) will acquire Assist America, a provider of global emergency travel assistance services, the Bermuda-based (re)insurer said.

** Private healthcare operator Akdital <AKT.CS> has agreed to buy Tunisia's Taoufik Hospitals Group for $90 million, the Moroccan company said.

** Azerbaijan's state energy company SOCAR signed a deal to buy a 870 MW gas-fired power plant in central Turkey from Gama Enerji for $225 million, the company said.

** Samsung Electronics <005930.KS> announced a $1.8 billion deal to acquire Germany's ZF Friedrichshafen's autonomous driving technology unit to combine it with its audio business and offer next-generation in-car platforms.

** The Australian Retirement Trust (ART) will acquire a near 20% stake in Scentre Group's <SCG.AX> iconic Westfield Sydney shopping mall for A$864 million ($575.68 million), the shopping centre owner said.

data.title: US health agency to expand access to GLP-1 weight-loss drugs
data.description: The U.S. Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services said on Tuesday it will negotiate drug pricing with manufacturers of GLP-1 medications to expand access to the drugs that help with weight loss.

data.title: Velo3D Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
Velo3D announced a private placement supported by two material agreements. The company signed a Securities Purchase Agreement with institutional accredited investors to sell 3,636,363 common shares at $8.25 per share, including resale registration rights and post-effectiveness issuance and variable rate restrictions. Velo3D also executed an Engagement Letter with Lake Street Capital Markets and Lucid Capital Markets as placement agents, agreeing to a 6.0% cash fee and $64,000 in expenses. The offering closed on December 23, 2025, bolstering the company's liquidity.

Agreement 1: Velo3D Signs Securities Purchase Agreement With Institutional Accredited Investors

  • Agreement type: Securities purchase agreement for private placement of common stock
  • Counterparty: Institutional Accredited Investors
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 22 2025 / Dec 23 2025
  • Duration / Termination: One-time transaction
  • Reason: Raise capital and enhance liquidity

Agreement 2: Velo3D Signs Placement Agent Engagement Letter With Lake Street Capital Markets and Lucid Capital Markets

  • Agreement type: Placement agent engagement letter for private placement
  • Counterparty: Lake Street Capital Markets and Lucid Capital Markets
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 18 2025 / Dec 18 2025
  • Duration / Termination: Through closing of the private placement
  • Reason: Engage agents to execute the offering

Original SEC Filing: Velo3D, Inc. [ VELO ] - 8-K - Dec. 23, 2025

data.title: Amplify Energy Files 8K - Regulation FD >AMPY
data.description:
Amplify Energy Corp. (AMPY) filed a Form 8K - Regulation FD Disclosure - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on December 23, 2025.

On December 23, 2025, the Company issued a press release announcing the events described in Item 1.01 of this Current Report. A copy of the press release is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.2.

The information contained in this Item 7.01 shall not be deemed to be "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the "Exchange Act"), or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section, and shall not be deemed to be incorporated by reference into any of the Company's filings under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, whether made before or after the date hereof and regardless of any general incorporation language in such filings, except to the extent expressly set forth by specific reference in such a filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1533924/000110465925124255/tm2533464d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1533924/000110465925124255/0001104659-25-124255-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Amplify Energy Files 8K - Asset Acquisition Or Disposition >AMPY
data.description:
Amplify Energy Corp. (AMPY) filed a Form 8K - Acquisition or Disposition of Assets - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on December 23, 2025.

data.title: The Most Expensive Home for Sale on Shelter Island Asks $17.995 Million — WSJ
data.description:
By Libertina Brandt

For years, interior designer Ben Brougham had admired the Modernist Osofsky House on New York's Shelter Island, where he has vacationed for about 15 years. In fact, he said, the first thing he ever pinned on the photo-sharing website Pinterest was a picture of the waterfront Osofsky House, which was designed around 1970 by the late architect Norman Jaffe.

In 2020, Brougham purchased the Osofsky House with his then-partner, British media entrepreneur and politician Waheed Alli. They paid $7.15 million to buy it from its original owners, the Osofsky family, according to property records. Brougham, the senior creative director at the home-furnishings company Jonathan Adler, then spent two years renovating the six-bedroom house.

Now, the home is going back on the market for $17.995 million, making it Shelter Island's most expensive listing. If it fetches its asking price, the home will set a record for the island, according to listing agent Nick Brown with Sotheby's International Realty.

Accessed by ferry, Shelter Island is a summer getaway for affluent New Yorkers and is typically quieter than the Hamptons, according to Brown.

The Osofsky House sits on about 1.7 acres overlooking Gardiner's Bay. Jaffe designed the home as a family retreat for Mike and Aileen Osofsky. In the 1980s, the Osofskys asked Jaffe again to design an addition.

Brougham kept the original footprint of the roughly 5,100-square-foot home. While he said he "didn't mess with the core architectural finishes, " he reimagined several rooms to better suit entertaining. For example, he moved the kitchen from the second floor to the ground level to create more space for socializing, and put a bar in the original kitchen location. Brougham declined to disclose how much was spent on the renovation.

The home's guest wing, which was part of the 1980s addition, has a sunken living room. "It's one of my favorite cozy areas," said Brougham. "It's actually where I end up going in the winter to watch TV and snuggle up in the evening."

The 1.7-acre property had a pool and tennis court when Brougham and Alli bought it. They added a pergola with a fireplace, kitchen and dining area.

Brougham, who lives primarily in New York City, said the Osofsky House was used as a weekend and summer retreat.

The home's listing price in due in part to its architectural significance and move-in-ready condition, said Brown, who shares the listing with colleague Lidia Murphy.

The current Shelter Island record holder is a waterfront home on approximately 3.4 acres that sold for $12.95 million in 2023.

data.title: Suspect dead after Delaware state trooper killed in shooting
data.description:
A suspect is dead after a police officer was killed in a shooting in Wilmington, Delaware, police said on Tuesday.

Delaware State Police reported an active shooter at the Department of Motor Vehicles building in Wilmington just after 2 p.m. EST (1900 GMT). Shortly after, police said there was no longer an active shooter and that a suspect had been apprehended.

"One Delaware State Trooper has been confirmed killed during this incident. We are continuing to assess additional injuries," the state police force said on X.

Later, police said the suspect was dead and continued to advise people to avoid the area.

data.description:

data.title: Sabre Corp Announces Final Results of Exchange Offers
data.description:
On December 22, 2025, Sabre Corp announced the final results of its exchange offers for senior secured notes.

Key Highlights:

  • Sabre GLBL issued $1.43 million in new 10.750% notes due 2030.
  • All outstanding 8.625% and 11.250% notes due 2027 will be redeemed.
  • Exchange offers expired on December 19, 2025, with no late tenders accepted.
  • BofA Securities acted as Sole Dealer Manager for the offers.
  • Redemption of old notes scheduled for early 2026.

data.title: CVB Financial Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
CVB Financial announced a definitive Agreement and Plan of Reorganization and Merger with Heritage Commerce, under which Heritage will merge into CVB Financial and Heritage Bank of Commerce will merge into Citizens Business Bank. Heritage shareholders will receive 0.65 CVB Financial shares for each Heritage share, with the deal subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals. To support approvals and integration, the parties also executed Voting and Support Agreements with directors of both companies, and Non-Solicitation and Non-Disclosure Agreements with certain Heritage directors and executives.

Agreement 1: CVB Financial Signs Agreement And Plan Of Reorganization And Merger With Heritage Commerce

  • Agreement type: Agreement and Plan of Reorganization and Merger
  • Counterparty: Heritage Commerce
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 17 2025 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing; Outside Date Jan 15 2027
  • Reason: Expand scale and combine complementary banking franchises

Agreement 2: CVB Financial Signs Voting And Support Agreements With Heritage Directors

  • Agreement type: Voting and Support Agreements
  • Counterparty: Heritage Directors and CVB Financial Directors
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 17 2025 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: Until shareholder votes
  • Reason: Secure shareholder support for the merger

Agreement 3: CVB Financial Signs Non-Solicitation And Non-Disclosure Agreements With Heritage Directors And Officers

  • Agreement type: Non-Solicitation and Non-Disclosure Agreements
  • Counterparty: Certain Heritage Directors; Thomas Sa and Clay Jones
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 17 2025 / N/A
  • Duration / Termination: 12 months post-closing (Jones: up to 12 months)
  • Reason: Protect relationships and ensure smooth post-merger integration

Original SEC Filing: CVB FINANCIAL CORP [ CVBF ] - 8-K - Dec. 23, 2025

Disclaimer

data.title: Aspira Women's Health Signs Multiple Financing Agreements
data.description:
Aspira Women's Health signed two related financing agreements with Lincoln Park Capital Fund to strengthen its liquidity. The company entered into a committed equity purchase facility of up to $10.0 million over 24 months, allowing Aspira to direct periodic share purchases by Lincoln Park at market-based prices with a 5% discount. As consideration, Aspira issued commitment shares equal to 3% of the facility size. A separate Registration Rights Agreement requires the company to register for resale the shares issued and issuable under the facility, enabling drawdowns once the SEC declares the registration effective.

Agreement 1: Aspira Women's Health Signs Equity Purchase Agreement With Lincoln Park Capital Fund

  • Agreement type: Equity purchase agreement (committed equity facility up to $10.0 million)
  • Counterparty: Lincoln Park Capital Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 23 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 24 months
  • Reason: Enhance liquidity with flexible equity financing

Agreement 2: Aspira Women's Health Signs Registration Rights Agreement With Lincoln Park Capital Fund

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement for resale registration
  • Counterparty: Lincoln Park Capital Fund
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 23 2025 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until resale registration remains effective
  • Reason: Enable resales under equity facility

Original SEC Filing: Aspira Women's Health Inc. [ AWHL ] - 8-K - Dec. 23, 2025

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
